b: ^Sarah: Hi! You must be the IT contractor. I'm Sarah, the receptionist.
^Sarah: Let me get you checked in.

first_checkin: ^Thanks. I'm here to audit your network security
^Just point me to IT and I'll get started

c-0: ^Sarah: Oh good! Kevin mentioned you'd be coming.
^Sarah: Let me print your visitor badge.
c-1: ^Sarah: Sure thing. Let me get your badge first.

receive_badge: ^Sarah: Here you go. This gets you into public areas.
^Sarah: Restricted areas need keycard access or you'll need to ask Kevin.
hub: ^Can you tell me about the office layout?
^Anyone working late I should know about?
ask_kevin_location: ^Sarah: Kevin's desk is in the main office area—can't miss it. Covered in monitors and coffee cups.
^Sarah: He's usually there this time of day.
kevin_personality: ^Sarah: Super helpful, kind of overworked. The company relies on him way too much.
^Sarah: He'll appreciate having someone competent help out.
ask_office_layout: ^Sarah: Main office is through there—hot-desking setup. Conference room on the west side, break room to the east.
^Sarah: Server room is behind main office, but you'll need Kevin's access for that.
ask_executive_offices: ^Sarah: Derek's office is off the main area—he's our Senior Marketing Manager. Usually locks his door when he's out.
^Sarah: Most people just have desk space, but Derek got an office because of client confidentiality stuff.
ask_late_workers: ^Sarah: Derek's usually here late. Like, really late. Sometimes I leave at 6 and he's still working.
^Sarah: He says it's because of client timezones, but...
derek_suspicion: ^Sarah: I don't know. It just seems weird, you know? He's marketing, not IT.
^Sarah: And I've seen him in the server room a couple times. Told me he was checking on campaign servers.

c-0: ^Sarah: Right? But I'm just the receptionist. What do I know?
c-1: ^Sarah: Maybe. Anyway, Kevin would know more about the technical stuff.
